purpose: Craft examples for chase, clash, blocking, impact, and clean action beat handoff without turning action into random chaos.
source_priority_rules: Source text and locked payload outrank this example pack.
Use examples only to shape beat readability, spacing, impact, and reaction timing.
Do not invent combat logic that the source never establishes.
genre_boundary: Use visible start, contact, reaction, and recover states.
Keep motion readable so actions can be storyboarded and cut cleanly.
Do not turn an action beat into a generic explosion or random fight.

scene_card_examples.visual: The locked lead steps into frame, sets stance, and blocks the first source-grounded attack in a readable line.
scene_card_examples.voiceover: The fight is not wild; it has a clear start, impact, and recovery.

asset_goal.purpose: clear stance, contact line, and spatial axis

script_scene_examples.visual: A chase or clash resolves into a visible stop point so the next beat can re-enter the space cleanly.
script_scene_examples.voiceover: The movement matters because the space changes with it.

dialogue.speaker: locked lead
dialogue.line: Now I know where you stand.

script_scene_examples.action_result: The action ends on a readable new position or advantage.

dialogue_style_examples: Use short lines that reveal timing, distance, or intent.
Keep action dialogue tied to a visible change, not a slogan.

negative_examples.bad: Turn the action into a random barrage with no stance or result.
negative_examples.why_bad: It breaks readability and downstream continuity.

forbidden_drift: Do not swap lead character identity, role hierarchy, or speaker authority during action without source support.
Do not introduce new props, weapons, or tools just to make the fight longer.
Do not change room, route, door, threshold, or blocking position without a visible causal transition.
Do not alter relationship status, knowledge state, or alliance state mid-clash without source support.
Do not change cause and effect so the action appears out of nowhere.

image: Lock body stance, hand used for the weapon or prop, and the visible contact line.
video: Preserve start -> impact -> recover continuity so the clip can be cut and bridged cleanly.
